food_subgroup: Noodle dishes with coconut milk broth

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for coconut milk noodle. FOODON_03301710 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Noodles, cooked) is the closest parent; no specific coconut milk noodle code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 (Noodles) is the closest available parent concept; no specific coconut milk noodle concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one bowl (350 g), comprising noodles, coconut milk broth, protein topping, and garnishes.

nutrient_source.title: Mohinga / Coconut Milk Noodle Soup — nutrient profile estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table and USDA Foundation Foods component data for coconut milk, wheat noodles, chicken, and vegetables
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table; USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for 'Coconut Milk Noodle' as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition: approximately 45% cooked wheat noodles, 30% coconut milk broth (diluted), 15% chicken/fish protein, 10% vegetables and egg, based on FAO/INFOODS SEA component data and USDA Foundation Foods. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate for a typical 100 g portion of the assembled dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat noodles and coconut milk composite — analogous estimate based on 'Noodles, wheat, boiled' (GI 52) from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Coconut Milk Noodle as a composite dish. GI of 52 is adopted from 'Wheat noodles, boiled' (GI 52, University of Sydney GI Database). Coconut milk fat content is expected to modestly lower the effective GI relative to plain noodles; GI 52 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving calculated as (52 × 50.75 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 26.4, rounded to 26.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~1120 mg per bowl)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bowl and request reduced fish sauce or soy sauce to keep sodium intake within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~26.5 per bowl) driven by refined wheat noodle carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should choose a smaller portion (approximately 200 g), pair with additional non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial glucose, as the moderate glycemic load of a full bowl may cause a meaningful blood glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (~5.1 g per 100 g; ~17.9 g per bowl)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional servings, as the high saturated fat content from coconut milk may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Milk Noodle (known in Malay as Mee Santan, and in Burmese as အုန်းနို့ခေါက်ဆွဲ) is a Southeast Asian noodle dish in which wheat noodles or rice vermicelli are served in a rich, aromatic broth made from coconut milk, lemongrass, turmeric, garlic, and onion. The dish is commonly topped with shredded chicken or flaked fish, a hard-boiled egg, bean sprouts, and spring onion. The broth is creamy, mildly spiced, and fragrant, with a characteristic sweetness from the coconut milk balanced by the savouriness of fish sauce. In Singapore, it is served at hawker centres and cafes, often as a breakfast or lunch item. Nutritionally, a standard bowl (approximately 350 g) provides around 483 kcal, with moderate protein (~21.7 g), significant saturated fat from coconut milk (~17.9 g), and a moderate glycemic load (~26.5). The high saturated fat content warrants moderation for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, while the moderate sodium level (~1120 mg per bowl) is a concern for those with hypertension. For people with Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate glycemic load from refined noodles requires portion control and pairing with fibre-rich vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.
common_user_queries: How many calories in coconut milk noodle?
Is coconut milk noodle suitable for diabetics?
What are the ingredients in coconut milk noodle?
Is coconut milk noodle high in saturated fat?
What is the GI of coconut milk noodle?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Coconut Milk Noodle as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA Foundation Foods component data using a weighted ingredient model. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using wheat noodles (GI 52) as the closest defensible reference. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag in the input JSON was listed as 'Italian', which is clearly erroneous for a Southeast Asian coconut milk noodle dish; corrected to 'Singaporean / Southeast Asian'. 2. The food name in the input includes a Burmese script parenthetical (အုန်းနို့ခေါက်ဆွဲ), indicating a Burmese-origin dish (Ohn No Khao Swe) served in a Singapore context; both cultural contexts are reflected. 3. Nutrient values are estimated from component data; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore Foodo entry would upgrade confidence to high. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as coconut milk and the other natural ingredients in this dish do not contain industrially produced trans fats; any trace amounts are below detection threshold. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA Foundation Foods component data used for this estimation. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA component data used for this estimation. 7. glycemic_index set to 52 (analogous estimate from wheat noodles, boiled, University of Sydney GI Database); gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule.
